osc_decision.reason_1_closure_floods: translation-part neighbor automaton, decoupled from the side-state, reaches delta=0 (root coincidence) and saturates the ENTIRE lattice ball -> menu-closure coincidence set NON-empty -> OSC fails on the closure; min nonzero Q(delta)=1 (lattice quantum) does NOT separate arcs of extent rho_c~27
osc_decision.reason_2_relative_gap_to_zero: realized-walk min sibling gap pinned at Cheb=1 (absolute) for gen 1/2/3; relative gap rho0 = 1.0, 0.3333, 0.1111 = 3^{-(g-1)} -> inf of relative sibling separations = 0 -> Bandt-Graf positive-separation criterion NOT met; finite-depth gap is O(3^{-h}) = same order as tail rho_c*3^{-h} -> positivity undecidable at any finite depth (near-overlap boundary)

incidence_locality.explanation: sigma (78728 NP-HC state) determines the LEGAL connector menu, so on the menu-closure incidence is a function of the finite local state. But the REALIZED connector word is picked by the GLOBAL triple-free/shortest-legal rule -> realized subsystem is a sofic/S-adic subshift, not clean finite type; the decoupled automaton flooding to delta=0 is concrete proof separation is NOT a function of the translation type alone

refill_incidence_bound_level_uniform.level_uniform_because: neighbor types are level-free -> max incidence over the finite type set is a level-uniform constant by construction

dimension.exact_perron_not_computed: sharp stitch-incidence matrix T needs full-sigma neighbor-type resolution; only measured lambda delivered here
